agent.name: Ascend SAP PR Agent
agent.description: Conversational SAP ECC 6.0 Purchase Requisition lifecycle agent for Ascend Performance Materials. Supports PR creation, approval, cancellation, status-check, and vendor lookup — powered by Dataverse (demo) or live SAP ERP connector (production).
agent.author: Microsoft Mfg CoE

metadata.name: AscendSAPPR
metadata.description: SAP Purchase Requisition lifecycle management for Ascend Performance Materials.

scripted_demo.name: Ascend SAP PR Lifecycle Demo
scripted_demo.persona: Sarah Chen, Procurement Manager at Ascend Performance Materials
scripted_demo.context: Ascend is a global chemicals manufacturer. Sarah needs to procure a new industrial compressor from a vetted SAP vendor. The demo walks through the full SAP PR lifecycle — vendor lookup, PR creation with DoA approval routing, status check, and approval — all via natural language in Microsoft Teams.

steps.title: Introduction
steps.user_prompt: Hi, I'm Sarah from Procurement at Ascend. Can you help me raise a purchase requisition for some new equipment?
steps.agent_response:
**Hello Sarah! 👋 I'm the Ascend SAP Procurement Assistant.**

I can help you with the full Purchase Requisition lifecycle in SAP ECC 6.0:

📋 **What I can do:**
- 🔍 Look up approved SAP vendors (LFA1/LFB1)
- ✏️ Create a PR — I'll auto-route to the right approver based on your DoA level
- ✅ Approve or reject PRs in your queue
- 📊 Check PR status (EBAN)
- ❌ Cancel or edit open PRs
- 🔔 Send reminders to approvers

Let's get started! **What equipment do you need, and roughly what's the budget?**
talking_points: Note the native Teams interface — no SAP GUI needed
Agent understands SAP terminology (EBAN, LFA1, DoA) automatically
Available 24/7, responds in seconds vs. navigating SAP ME51N transaction

steps.title: Vendor Lookup
steps.user_prompt: I need an industrial compressor for our Texas City plant. Budget is around $85,000. Can you check if Siemens is an approved vendor?
steps.agent_response:
🔍 **Searching SAP vendor master (LFA1/LFB1/LFM1)...**

✅ **Siemens AG** is an approved vendor:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Vendor ID (LIFNR)** | 1000001 |
| **Name** | Siemens AG |
| **Company Code** | 1000 (Ascend US) |
| **Purchasing Org** | 1000 |
| **Currency** | USD |
| **Status** | ✅ Active — no purchasing block |

> 📌 *Demo: Data from Dataverse `ascend_sapvendor` table, emulating SAP LFA1/LFB1/LFM1. In production, queries live SAP via RFC_READ_TABLE.*

**Ready to create the PR?** I'll need:
- Item description
- Material group / category code
- GL account
- Cost center
- Payment cadence (one-time, monthly, quarterly, yearly)
talking_points: Vendor lookup emulates SAP LFA1/LFB1/LFM1 — same data model as live SAP
Checks purchasing block (SPERR/SPERM) automatically
In production: replaces SAP GUI vendor search (ME1M transaction)

steps.title: PR Creation with DoA Routing
steps.user_prompt: Great, let's create it. Item: Industrial Air Compressor - 250HP, category code 05 (Machinery), GL account 640010, cost center CC1001, one-time payment of $85,000.
steps.agent_response:
⚙️ **Creating SAP Purchase Requisition...**

Running pre-flight checks:
✅ Cost center CC1001 validated (CSKS)
✅ GL account 640010 confirmed (SKA1)
✅ DoA level calculated: **$85,000 → L2 Approval required**
✅ Contract match found: Framework agreement **4500012345** (Siemens, Machinery)

---

**✅ PR Created Successfully!**

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **PR Number (BANFN)** | 1002604210 |
| **Item** | Industrial Air Compressor - 250HP |
| **Vendor** | Siemens AG (1000001) |
| **Total Value** | $85,000.00 |
| **Framework Contract** | 4500012345 |
| **GL Account** | 640010 |
| **Cost Center** | CC1001 |
| **Status** | 🟡 Pending Approval |
| **Assigned Approver** | **Michael Torres** (L2 DoA) |

> 📌 *Demo: Written to Dataverse `ascend_sappurchaserequisition` (EBAN) + `ascend_sappraccountassignment` (EBKN). In production: BAPI_PR_CREATE writes to SAP EBAN + EBKN.*

**Michael Torres has been notified.** Want me to send him a reminder, or check the status later?
talking_points: DoA routing is automatic — no manual approver lookup in SAP T16FS
Framework contract matching happens in real-time (EKKO)
Both EBAN and EBKN records created in one conversational turn
Approver notification built-in — replaces manual email chase

steps.title: Status Check
steps.user_prompt: Can you check the status of PR 1002604210?
steps.agent_response:
📊 **PR Status — SAP EBAN Lookup**

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **PR Number** | 1002604210 |
| **Description** | Industrial Air Compressor - 250HP |
| **Vendor** | Siemens AG |
| **Value** | $85,000.00 |
| **Created** | April 21, 2026 |
| **Status** | 🟡 **Pending L2 Approval** |
| **Approver** | Michael Torres |
| **Contract** | 4500012345 |

> 📌 *Demo: Real-time read from Dataverse `ascend_sappurchaserequisition`. In production: RFC_READ_TABLE / BAPI_REQUISITION_GETDETAIL on EBAN.*

Still waiting on Michael. Want me to **send him a reminder**?
talking_points: Real-time status — no SAP GUI ME53N transaction needed
Works from mobile via Teams
Links to contract and approver in one response

steps.title: Send Approval Reminder
steps.user_prompt: Yes, please send Michael a reminder.
steps.agent_response:
🔔 **Approval Reminder Sent**

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **To** | Michael Torres (michael.torres@ascend.com) |
| **Subject** | Action Required: PR 1002604210 Awaiting L2 Approval |
| **PR Value** | $85,000.00 — Industrial Air Compressor |
| **SLA** | Response required within 48 hours |

> 📌 *Demo: Emulates SAP BUS2105 workflow reminder (SWI_CREATE_VIA_INTERNET). In production: re-triggers SAP workflow work item or sends via Exchange.*

✅ Michael has been notified. I'll track this for you — want me to **escalate to his manager after 48 hours** if still open?
talking_points: Automated reminder replaces manual follow-up emails
Emulates SAP BUS2105 workflow notification pattern
Escalation paths configurable — no custom ABAP needed

steps.title: Approval (Approver View)
steps.user_prompt: I'm Michael Torres. I've reviewed PR 1002604210 — looks good, I'll approve it.
steps.agent_response:
✅ **PR Approved — SAP EBAN Updated**

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **PR Number** | 1002604210 |
| **Action** | ✅ **Approved** by Michael Torres |
| **Approval Level** | L2 DoA |
| **Timestamp** | April 21, 2026 18:07 UTC |
| **New Status** | 🟢 **Approved — Ready for PO** |

> 📌 *Demo: Dataverse `ascend_sappurchaserequisition.ascend_frgst` updated to 100001 (Approved). In production: BAPI_PR_CHANGE updates SAP EBAN release indicator (FRGST).*

**Sarah Chen has been notified** — her PR is approved and ready for PO conversion (ME21N / automatic via output type).

🎉 **Full PR lifecycle complete in under 3 minutes — without opening SAP GUI once.**
talking_points: Approver can act from Teams without SAP access
FRGST (release indicator) updated in real-time
Requester auto-notified — closes the loop
Key value prop: SAP PR lifecycle in natural language, mobile-friendly, 24/7

closing_value_props: **Time saved:** PR lifecycle from 45 min (SAP GUI) to under 5 min (Teams chat)
**No SAP GUI training:** Procurement staff use natural language
**Mobile-ready:** Full PR management from Teams on any device
**Auto-compliance:** DoA routing, vendor validation, contract matching — automatic
**Production path:** Swap Dataverse connector for SAP ERP connector — same flows, same bot
**Extensible:** Add GR/IR matching, invoice reconciliation, spend analytics in Phase 2

objection_handlers.objection: We already have SAP Fiori for self-service
objection_handlers.response: Fiori still requires SAP authentication and training. This runs in Teams where procurement staff already live — no context switch, no VPN, works on mobile.

objection_handlers.objection: How do we handle the SAP integration in production?
objection_handlers.response: The Power Automate flows use the SAP ERP connector with on-premises data gateway. We swap the Dataverse actions for SAP connector actions — same flow structure, same bot, no Copilot Studio changes needed.
Every action writes to Dataverse with timestamp and user GUID. In production, SAP's standard change documents (CDHDR/CDPOS) provide the audit trail. We can surface both in the bot.
